title: [Click eStock] "GS Engineering & Construction: 10,000 Units Pre-Sold in H1... Target Price Raised" - asiae.co.kr
content:
[Click eStock] "GS Engineering & Construction: 10,000 Units Pre-Sold in H1... Target Price Raised" On April 8, Hana Securities raised its target price for GS Engineering & Construction to 39,000 won, reflecting the pre-sale of 10,000 units in the first half of the year. The investment opinion remains 'buy'. Kim Seungjun, a researcher at Hana Securities, stated in a report released on this day, "So far, there appears to be no impact from the strait closure," adding, "The Saudi Fadhili project is proceeding without interruption, and there is no need at this stage to consider alternative routes for materials." GS Engineering & Construction's sales for the first quarter of this year are estimated at 2.7 trillion won, a decrease of 9.7% compared to the previous year, while operating profit is expected to increase by 41.3% to 99.5 billion won. The company pre-sold approximately 4,400 units in the first quarter, with 6,400 units planned for the second quarter. First-quarter orders are projected to be 3.5 trillion won. Researcher Kim noted, "Future estimates depend on whether the strait closure is prolonged, and if the war drags on, it will become increasingly difficult to procure building materials or if high oil prices persist, an increase in construction costs and other cost rises will be inevitable. The overall margin estimates could be revised downward, and the timing of the housing market turnaround may be delayed due to higher construction costs." © The Asia Business Daily(www.asiae.co.kr). title: BMW F 450 GS India Launch Date Revealed - bikewale.com
content: BMW Motorrad has teased the launch of the much-anticipated F 450 GS on its social media handles, hinting at an India debut on 23 April, 2026. The F 450 GS marks a significant step forward for BMW in the entry-level ADV space, as it will replace the now-discontinued BMW G 310 GS in India. While the motorcycle has been designed and developed by BMW Motorrad in Germany, it will be exclusively manufactured in India by TVS Motor Company at its Hosur facility. Powering the new BMW F 450 GS is a 420cc, liquid-cooled, parallel-twin engine that produces 48bhp at 8,750rpm and 43Nm of peak torque at 6,750rpm. The motor is paired with a six-speed gearbox and comes equipped with a slipper clutch and a bi-directional quickshifter as standard. In terms of design, the baby GS takes inspiration from the flagship BMW R 1300 GS, carrying forward the brand’s signature ADV styling in a more compact and accessible form factor. On the hardware front, the F 450 GS is expected to feature long-travel suspension with USD front forks and a monoshock at the rear. It will offer adjustability to tune it for on-road as well as off-road riding conditions. The bike will ride on a 19-inch front and 17-inch rear wheel setup, wrapped in dual-purpose tyres. BMW is also expected to offer the F 450 GS in four variants - Basic, Exclusive, Sport, and GS Trophy, with the latter being a more off-road-focused trim with additional equipment such as tubeless wire-spoke wheels, different tyres, and enhanced electronic aids. The F 450 GS will be positioned as a premium offering in the sub-500cc ADV segment, and is expected to be priced upwards of Rs. 5 lakh (ex-showroom). Once launched, it will go up against rivals like the KTM 390 Adventure and the Royal Enfield Himalayan 450, both of which are well-established bikes in the category.

title: GS Caltex Veolia Sustainability Partnership | News - Chemical Industry Digest
content:
GS Caltex has signed a comprehensive Memorandum of Understanding (MOU) with Veolia, a global leader in environmental solutions, to enhance sustainability and modernise utility operations at its Yeosu Complex in South Korea. The agreement, announced by Saehong Hur, Board Chair and CEO of GS Caltex and Vice Chairman of GS Group, marks an important step toward improving energy efficiency, environmental performance, and digital transformation at one of the company’s major industrial facilities. Expanding Collaboration with a Global Environmental Leader The partnership was formalised on April 3 following the visit of French President Emmanuel Macron to South Korea, reflecting stronger cooperation between the two countries in sustainable technologies. Until now, GS Caltex and Veolia had collaborated mainly on specific water treatment projects. However, the new agreement expands the relationship into a broader strategic partnership focused on long-term sustainability initiatives. GS Caltex and Veolia plan to jointly develop integrated environmental, energy, and digital solutions. The collaboration will follow a Triple-X transformation framework, which includes Business Transformation (BX), Digital and AI Transformation (DAX) and Green Transformation (GX).  Together, these initiatives aim to optimise operational efficiency, accelerate digital innovation, and strengthen environmental sustainability at the Yeosu Complex. Business Transformation to Optimise Utility Operations Under the Business Transformation (BX) pillar, the two companies will explore ways to integrate and optimise wastewater treatment operations within the Yeosu Complex. In addition, they will examine opportunities to enhance utility synergies with nearby industrial facilities, which could improve resource efficiency and reduce operational costs. By streamlining these processes, GS Caltex aims to increase productivity while strengthening its overall competitiveness. Digital and AI Transformation for Smart Plant Operations Digital innovation is another key focus of the partnership. Through the Digital & AI Transformation (DAX) initiative, Veolia will introduce its AI-powered digital solutions to improve operational management at the Yeosu Complex. These technologies will enable real-time monitoring and analysis of utility data, advanced process optimisation, early detection of operational anomalies and predictive maintenance systems. By implementing these solutions, GS Caltex plans to accelerate its transition toward a smart manufacturing plant powered by intelligent digital systems. The Green Transformation (GX) component of the partnership will focus on deploying eco-friendly technologies developed by Veolia to improve environmental performance. Key initiatives will include, wastewater reuse systems, upgrades to industrial cooling systems and resource recovery technologies. Through these measures, GS Caltex aims to reduce carbon emissions, conserve resources, and support the development of a circular economy. According to CEO Saehong Hur, the collaboration with Veolia will play a crucial role in transforming the company’s operations and advancing its environmental, social, and governance (ESG) strategy. He emphasised that the partnership will act as a catalyst for improving overall utility operations while elevating GS Caltex’s ESG management to a higher level. As per the press release, by combining Veolia’s global expertise in environmental solutions with GS Caltex’s industrial capabilities, the two companies aim to build a more sustainable, efficient, and technologically advanced energy and chemical production system at the Yeosu Complex. 1,true,6,Contact Email,21,false,1,First Name,21,false,1,Last Name,2 518, Crystal Paradise, Dattaji Salvi Marg, Off. title: BMW F700GS and F800GS (2013-2018) - Review & Buying Guide - bennetts.co.uk
content: In the late 2000s and early 2010s BMW were starting to take a lot of interest in off-road riding. Previously they had relied upon the boxer-powered GS models as well as the fairly limp Rotax-powered single-cylinder F650 machines but this period saw not only the launch of the new G-Series bikes (which flopped..) but also the radical G450X motocross bike (which also flopped...) and even the acquisition of Husqvarna (which flopped...) and then a few Husky-branded models based around BMW platforms (which flopped...). As you can see, it wasn’t exactly a successful foray into the world of mud and dirt... Brushing over the failures, one family did rise up and succeed - the F-Series models. Despite sharing a name with the previous generation of singles, the new generation of F-Series models were powered by an all-new parallel-twin engine and after the F800S debuted the motor in 2006 an F800R and F800ST quickly followed before in 2011 BMW released two GS models. Slightly confusingly named (more on this later, it’s an F-Series trend...) the F800GS and F650GS took the GS name in noticeably different directions with the F800GS a dual-sports bike with genuine off-road ability and the F650GS very much a tarmac-targeted machine with adventure bike styling. In 2013 both F-Series GS models were revised and enhanced in their tech-level and while the F800GS retained its name, the F650GS became the F700GS – despite no change in capacity! It’s a bit of a numerically confusing family of bikes to say the least... When the two GS models were launched the F800GS commanded a £1000 premium over the F700GS and this trend continues in the used market, which is surprising because generally the F800GS is the more common machine as the F700GS failed to sell in anything like as large numbers. The lower end of the market for both models is £2999 for a well-used example with £3999 getting you a really good F700GS and £4500-£5000 about the price range for a tidy F800GS,ideally with panniers included. Interestingly, the F700GS’s price tops out at £4999 where the F800GS pushes its maximum cost to a higher £5999. It’s all about desirability and the more rugged model generally catches buyers’ eyes far more than the road-targeted variant. Here is where it can get a bit confusing. Both the F800GS and F700GS (as well as the F650GS before it) are powered by the same 798cc parallel-twin motor which features a 360-degree crank to give it an even firing order and feel and sound like a boxer twin (this has been changed on the modern F-Series models). It is a bit of a silly naming convention but BMW wanted the road-targeted bike to be aimed more at newer riders and as such disguised its actual capacity so as not to intimidate. Although it’s not quite as straightforward as that... Despite the engine being identical in its design between the two models, a different tune (mainly cams and fuelling) sees the F800GS produce 85bhp with 83Nm of torque where the F700GS makes 75bhp with 77Nm of torque. And this matters because when you ride the F700GS it can feel a touch lacklustre and disappointing where the F800GS is considerably peppier and more spirited to ride, helped by a deliberately spikey throttle response to give it some extra zing with the F700GS’s throttle action deliberately more muted. Both bikes actually sound pretty good thanks to the even firing order but again, the F800GS has a more abrupt edge to its exhaust note than the F700GS. Interestingly, both GS models feature a chain final drive where other F-Series bikes have a belt drive system, something BMW changed to suit the off-road abilities of the GS range. With all this in mind, it has to be said the F-Series parallel twin is a very good engine with strong mid-range, impressive economy figures and an excellent reliability record. As with all new motors, initially there were a few teething issues but for the 2013-onwards bikes these were ironed out and while BMW only claimed small tweaks to optimise power and torque efficiency as well as a shorter overall gear ratio on the F700GS (one tooth less on its rear sprocket), the changes were slightly more in depth than this. When buying used service history is fairly important as the valve-clearances need checking every 12,000 miles (lots of owners stretch this to 24,000 without issues) and also the state of its electrics as reg/recs fail and so do generators. To be fair neither is the end of the world – as long as they don’t cause damage to other electrical systems... Aside from this, it is the usual stuff such as ensuring the gearbox feels precise, there are no signs of smoke on start-up and the clutch isn’t slipping. While the 2013-onwards bike are fairly similar engine-wise to the original generation, when it comes to their chassis and tech level, the changes are considerably more substantial. And this is why we’d recommend buying a 2013-onwards model rather than an early 2011-2012 generation. So what’s different? Starting with the basics, the F800GS retains a 21in front wheel were the F700GS has a 19in front, which was also the case with the F800GS compared to the F650GS. But at the front the F700GS gains an extra brake disc over the F650GS (it does need it) and also both bikes come with ABS as standard, where it wasn’t an option on the F650GS, only the F800GS. And that’s not the only electrical addition. Electronic Suspension Adjustment (ESA) was introduced in 2013 on both bikes as an optional extra. Only on the shock, it is a basic system which alters the rebound damping’s level to one of three settings (Comfort, Normal or Sport) and nothing else, spring preload remains manually-adjustable. It is also worth noting at this point that the F800GS comes with inverted forks where the F700GS has lower-spec conventional units, which alongside its cast wheels (the F800GS has spoke wheels) give it a slightly budget look. Both bikes run the same two-piston sliding calipers, which are acceptable in their performance. Handling-wise, the F700GS is noticeably more secure and planted on the road thanks to its shorter travel suspension and 19in front wheel (there are also geometry changes between the two bikes) and if you aren’t interested in off-road riding, it is the model to pick. Yes, the F800GS has more kerb appeal and poke, but the F700GS is a better machine for commuting or light touring duties. That said, arm the F800GS’s 21in front wheel with modern rubber and the gap between them isn’t as big as it was back in the day thanks to advancements in modern adventure rubber. In the used market the main area of concern is wear and tear with things such as wheel bearings, head stock bearings and suspension all starting to show its age by now. On the F800GS always check the condition of its spoke wheels (the rims are aluminium and generally the spokes don’t rust) and on both bikes be wary of any signs of damage. The F800GS has quite a tall seat height, so low speed drops can be an issue, where the F700GS is favoured by newer riders, so again it isn’t uncommon to find out it has been on its side... Due to slight variations in final gearing (internal ratios are identical), BMW claimed the F700GS was marginally more economical than the F800GS. But in all honestly, the differences are very marginal indeed. You can expect to average in the range of 50mpg – 65mpg on both bikes with around 59mpg average. That’s not bad at all and should see the 16L tank good for about 200 miles before you need to begin pushing... In terms of comfort, the F700GS is the easier machine for mile-munching but the F800GS isn’t bad – as long as you don’t mind the vibrations. A few owners grumble about the parallel-twin’s vibes becoming intrusive, something heavier bar end weights can help dampen out and also choosing more road-orientated rubber with a less chunky tread pattern. As you would expect on BMW models, both the F700GS and F800GS arrived with a range of optional extras. While ABS was standard fitment on both bikes, ESA was an option and so was ASC, BMW’s basic traction control system. Owners could also specify lowered suspension, which drops the seat height by 30mm on both bikes. Only the F700GS can have tyre pressure monitors fitted, the F800GS can’t, but both can have heated grips, luggage, crash protection etc. Generally speaking, most used bikes come with official BMW accessories with panniers, heated grips, ASC and ESA common in both models. Naturally you should always check the spec before you buy and with ESA, always take the bike on a test ride to ensure the servo within the shock is still varying the damping and there are no fault codes from either the ESA or ABS. When it comes to upper-middleweight dual sport bikes, the BMWs’ main rivals come from Triumph with the Tiger 800 models, which also arrived in road and off-road targeted variations. That said, middleweights such as the V-Strom 650, Transalp XL700V, Versys 650 and even Tenere XT660Z are worth considering for road commuting or light touring duties. If you are after a solid road bike, the F700GS is a better option than the F800GS as its 19in front wheel gives it superior road holding to the F800GS’s skinny 21in item. That said, there is no denying its lower-performance motor is a touch lacklustre compared to the sprightlier F800GS’s tune. It’s a hard one to call as modern rubber will help close the deficit in feel and grip levels and the F800GS comes with far more eye-catching looks than the F700GS, which is noticeably more subdued. Newer riders are advised to sway more towards the F700GS as it gives better tarmac riding confidence and has a lower seat height where those who genuinely think they might try out some green lane riding are better off opting for the F800GS – or even the more hardcore F800GS Adventure, which appeared a little while afterwards and has a larger tank... title: Rothschild & Co Redburn Lowers The Goldman Sachs Group (NYSE:GS) Price Target to $839.00 - MarketBeat
content:
The Goldman Sachs Group (NYSE:GS - Get Free Report) had its price objective lowered by research analysts at Rothschild & Co Redburn  from $843.00 to $839.00 in a report released on Tuesday,MarketScreener reports. The firm currently has a "neutral" rating on the investment management company's stock. Rothschild & Co Redburn's price objective would indicate a potential downside of 2.10% from the company's previous close. A number of other analysts also recently issued reports on GS. HSBC reduced their price objective on The Goldman Sachs Group from $802.00 to $729.00 in a research note on Wednesday, April 1st. Royal Bank Of Canada upped their price target on shares of The Goldman Sachs Group from $900.00 to $1,030.00 and gave the company a "sector perform" rating in a research note on Tuesday, January 20th. BMO Capital Markets lowered their price objective on shares of The Goldman Sachs Group from $985.00 to $905.00 and set a "market perform" rating for the company in a research report on Monday. Evercore reduced their target price on shares of The Goldman Sachs Group from $1,075.00 to $950.00 and set an "outperform" rating on the stock in a report on Monday. Finally, Jefferies Financial Group decreased their target price on shares of The Goldman Sachs Group from $1,125.00 to $1,049.00 and set a "buy" rating on the stock in a research report on Monday. Eight research analysts have rated the stock with a Buy rating and fourteen have issued  a Hold rating to the company's stock. According to MarketBeat, The Goldman Sachs Group  has a consensus rating of "Hold" and a consensus price target of $927.10. Shares of GS stock opened at $857.01 on Tuesday. The company's fifty day simple moving average is $873.60 and its two-hundred day simple moving average is $851.88. The Goldman Sachs Group has a 52 week low of $447.11 and a 52 week high of $984.70. The stock has a market cap of $252.81 billion, a P/E ratio of 16.68, a PEG ratio of 1.13 and a beta of 1.32. The company has a quick ratio of 0.66, a current ratio of 0.66 and a debt-to-equity ratio of 2.60. The Goldman Sachs Group (NYSE:GS - Get Free Report) last issued its quarterly earnings data on Thursday, January 15th. The investment management company reported $13.55 earnings per share for the quarter, beating analysts' consensus estimates of $11.52 by $2.03. The firm had revenue of $15.71 billion during the quarter, compared to analyst estimates of $14.30 billion. The Goldman Sachs Group had a return on equity of 15.72% and a net margin of 13.73%.The business's quarterly revenue was down 3.0% compared to the same quarter last year. During the same quarter last year, the business posted $11.95 earnings per share.  Analysts predict that  The Goldman Sachs Group will post 47.12 EPS for the current fiscal year. In other The Goldman Sachs Group news, insider Kathryn H. Ruemmler sold 9,589 shares of the business's stock in a transaction that occurred on Friday, January 23rd. The stock was sold at an average price of $927.51, for a total transaction of $8,893,893.39. Following the completion of the transaction, the insider owned 18,533 shares in the company, valued at $17,189,542.83. The trade was a 34.10% decrease in their ownership of the stock. The sale was disclosed in a filing with the Securities & Exchange Commission, which is available at this link. Also, Director David A. Viniar sold 45,000 shares of the company's stock in a transaction that occurred on Friday, January 16th. The shares were sold at an average price of $965.33, for a total value of $43,439,850.00. Following the transaction, the director directly owned 555,000 shares in the company, valued at approximately $535,758,150. This represents a 7.50% decrease in their position. The SEC filing for this sale provides additional information. Insiders sold 117,283 shares of company stock worth $112,016,033 in the last 90 days. Company insiders own  0.55% of the company's stock. A number of hedge funds and other institutional investors have recently bought and sold shares of GS. Hartline Investment Corp increased its holdings in  The Goldman Sachs Group by 153.3% during the third quarter. Hartline Investment Corp now owns 1,421 shares of the investment management company's stock valued at $1,132,000 after buying an additional 860 shares during the period.  A4 Wealth Advisors LLC purchased a new stake in  The Goldman Sachs Group during the third quarter worth $940,000.  Stevens Capital Management LP bought a new stake in  The Goldman Sachs Group during the 3rd quarter valued at $1,392,000.  Meridian Wealth Management LLC increased its holdings in shares of  The Goldman Sachs Group by 39.4% in the 3rd quarter. title: Woman Found Alive After She Went Missing for 4 Days in the Forest with No Food - Yahoo News New Zealand
content: G.S. Sharanya was rescued after getting lost during a trek to Tadiandamol peak in India She survived four days with no food, relying on a stream for drinking water Sharanya described the forest as beautiful and plans to continue hiking despite the ordeal A woman has been found alive after she went missing for four days in a jungle in India. On Sunday, April 5, Eshwar Khandre, the Minister for Forest, Ecology and Environment Minister of Karnataka, confirmed in a Facebook post that G.S. Sharanya had been located safely after getting lost during a “trek from Kerala to Tadiandamol peak in Kodagu district.” “The young woman has been detected by a total of nine teams, including Forest Department, Police Department, Naxal Nigraha Force, which have conducted day and night search operations [since] April 2,” he continued. The official added that Sharanya’s health was in good condition and that “arrangements have been made” to get her home. Never miss a story — sign up for PEOPLE's free daily newsletter to stay up-to-date on the best of what PEOPLE has to offer, from juicy celebrity news to compelling human interest stories. Sharanya, 36, told The Times of India that she got lost at around 10:40 a.m. on Thursday, when she and two others were descending from the peak. She said that after noticing the others resting, she decided she would wait for them. However, when she looked back again, they were gone. Sharanya then tried to climb back to the peak after noticing another group. But soon after, she could no longer see them and was lost. According to India Today, the hiker was part of a group with 15 others, as well as a nature guide. While she tried to walk down the hill due to the thickness of the forest, Shranya still couldn’t find anyone by the time it was 6:45 p.m. Despite getting lost, the IT professional said the forest had an atmosphere of companionship and beauty. “As darkness fell, the forest slowly came alive, first with the sound of cicadas and then with fireflies. It seemed like a full moon and the night was bright to the point that one couldn't sleep. It was the first time that I was seeing a night inside the forest, and it was beautiful," she told The Times of India. "I tried calling out, but I felt no one would hear me due to the sound of the flowing stream and the dense surroundings. The spot felt relatively safe, as it wasn't easily accessible for large animals. I was curious whether small animals would come to the stream and so watched for any movements," she added. During her time in the forest, Sharanya only had 18 ounces of water in her bottle and had finished her only food, a banana, when she reached the peak. She was able keep hydrated by drinking three liters of water from a stream. Sharanya recalled having leg pain and dealing with heavy rain during her time in the forest. She also encountered monkeys. Speaking about her rescue on the fourth day, she said she walked to the top of an open area when she “heard someone calling out.” After responding, “I realized they were locals. Since they hadn't spotted me yet, I shook a tree to signal my location.” Sharanya said that the incident did not ignite any fear in her when it comes to hiking, adding that she’s set to go on another trip to Tawang. title: UPSC Essentials | Mains answer practice — GS 3 : Questions on repo rate adjustments and principle of fast breeder reactors (Week 149) - The Indian Express
content: UPSC Essentials brings to you its initiative for the practice of Mains answer writing. It covers essential topics of static and dynamic parts of the UPSC Civil Services syllabus covered under various GS papers. This answer-writing practice is designed to help you as a value addition to your UPSC CSE Mains. Attempt today’s answer writing on questions related to topics of GS-3 to check your progress. 🚨 Click Here to read the UPSC Essentials magazine for March 2026. Share your views and suggestions in the comment box or at manas.srivastava@indianexpress.com🚨 In the context of the RBI Monetary Policy, discuss the effectiveness and limitations of repo rate adjustments as a tool to manage such supply-side inflation in India. Discuss the concept of ‘criticality’ and the working principle of fast breeder reactors. How this development strengthens India’s three-stage nuclear power programme? QUESTION 1: In the context of the RBI Monetary Policy, discuss the effectiveness and limitations of repo rate adjustments as a tool to manage such supply-side inflation in India. Relevance: The question links core macroeconomic concepts with current RBI policy actions. It is important for understanding policy trade-offs between growth and price stability, which can be asked in GS-3. Aspirants must also focus on ongoing challenges of managing inflation driven by external shocks like crude prices and geopolitics. Note: This is not a model UPSC answer. It only provides you with a thought process which you may incorporate into the answers. — As Reserve Bank of India Governor Sanjay Malhotra prepares to announce monetary policy, the economic landscape has evolved substantially since the February 2026 review: the West Asia conflict has obscured the economic vista. Rising crude oil prices and a raging conflict now offer new concerns, with the potential to drive up inflation and put corporate earnings and economic growth at risk due to rising input costs, supply disruption, and raw material shortages in numerous sectors. — A policy rate that remains unchanged would bring significant relief to borrowers, as their equivalent monthly instalments (EMIs) on home, vehicle, personal corporate, and small business loans are unlikely to alter. You may incorporate some of the following points in your answer: — People go to commercial banks either to deposit their savings or to get a loan. On savings/deposits, the bank pays interest to the depositor while on loans, it charges interest to the borrower. Typically, the interest rate banks charge on loans is higher than the interest they pay on deposits. This is how they make money. — The interest rate that the RBI charges when commercial banks borrow money from it is called the repo rate. The interest rate the central bank pays commercial banks when they park their excess cash is called the reverse repo rate. How repo rate adjustments as a tool manages inflation in India? — The RBI uses these two rates to set the tone for all other interest rates in the banking sector and, by extension, the broader economy. — For instance, when the RBI wants to encourage economic activity in the economy, it reduces the repo rates. Doing this enables commercial banks to bring down the interest rates they charge (on their loans) as well as the interest rate they pay on deposits. This, in turn, incentivises people to spend money, because keeping their savings in the bank now pays back a little less, and businesses are incentivised to take new loans for new investments because new loans now cost a little less as well. — On the other hand, when the RBI wants to control inflation, it increases the repo rate. Banks thus have to pay more interest to borrow from the RBI, which means they will charge more interest to their borrowers. At a macro level, this inhibits people from borrowing money as well as from spending, which in turn reduces the amount of money in the market, and thus negates inflation. — As the ongoing conflict in West Asia continues to disrupt global supply chains, shortages of critical inputs and rising uncertainty in international markets have heightened concerns among policymakers and industry stakeholders alike. In this context, the guidance and policy stance of the Governor and the MPC are set to be closely scrutinised, particularly with regard to their assessment of the inflation trajectory. — Against this backdrop, the central bank’s communication and policy measures will play a critical role in anchoring market expectations and ensuring financial stability amid an increasingly uncertain global environment. (Source: Monetary Policy: Why RBI will keep rates steady & revise inflation and growth forecasts, This Word Means: Repo Rate) How do external factors such as crude oil prices and geopolitical tensions constrain RBI’s policy choices? What are the causes of persistent high food inflation in India? Comment on the effectiveness of the monetary policy of the RBI to control this type of inflation. (2024) QUESTION 2: Discuss the concept of ‘criticality’ and the working principle of fast breeder reactors. How this development strengthens India’s three-stage nuclear power programme? Relevance: The topic integrates core nuclear physics concepts with India’s strategic energy policy. It is also linked to recent developments in India’s nuclear programme. Students should explore the dimensions of technological advancement, sustainability, and strategic autonomy. Note: This is not a model UPSC answer. It only provides you with a thought process which you may incorporate into the answers. — India is one of the few countries in the world with a long experience of developing nuclear technologies, including the generation of nuclear power. This includes a mastery over the pressurised heavy water reactor (PHWR) technology, or reactors that use natural uranium as fuel and heavy water (deuterium oxide) as coolant and moderator. — Two other technologies are a work-in-progress: atomic reactors called fast breeders and a longstanding project that aims at eventually fabricating thorium-based nuclear reactors. These three technologies (PHWRs-FBRs-Thorium reactors), progressing in series, make up India’s ambitious three-stage nuclear power programme. You may incorporate some of the following points in your answer: — The vital second stage of India’s three-stage nuclear programme got a boost with the country’s first indigenous Fast Breeder Reactor (FBR) at Kalpakkam in Tamil Nadu attaining criticality. Attaining criticality, or going critical, means the initiation of a self-sustaining nuclear fission reaction that will eventually lead to the generation of power by the 500-megawatt electric (MWe) FBR. — It is a “defining step” in advancing the country’s civil nuclear programme and said the indigenously designed and built reactor reflects “the depth of our scientific capability and the strength of our engineering enterprise”. Attaining criticality is a key milestone before full power generation, indicating that the reactor core is functioning as designed and that each fission event in the core now releases a sufficient number of neutrons to sustain an ongoing series of reactions. — In the second stage of India’s nuclear power programme, the FBRs would be deployed at scale. Fast reactors are essentially designed to produce more fuel than they consume and in the Indian context, ‘higher breeding’ is desired so that the rate at which the power capacity can grow would be higher. — FBRs enable the potential to harness the energy of natural uranium by over 60 times through multiple recycles. — These breeder reactors are also crucial for enlarging the inventory of plutonium — produced after the first stage PHWRs — so that a much larger irradiation capacity to produce an isotope of Uranium (U-233) at scale for use in the third stage programme can be built up. For this, at an appropriate stage, the FBRs would need to be loaded with thorium (Th232) as the blanket material which would be converted to U-233. With sufficient inventory and production capacity for U-233 having built up,the move onto the third stage can then happen. Thus, FBRs provide the essential link between the first and third stages of the power programme based on the indigenous nuclear material resources. — Once commissioned, India will be the second country after Russia to have a commercial operating FBR. China has a small programme on fast breeders; programmes in countries such as Japan, France, and the United States were shut down amid safety concerns. — The FBR is clearly being seen as an important milestone for getting to the third stage, clearing the way for the eventual full utilisation of the country’s thorium. Transitioning to thorium-based nuclear power generation in India is vital for securing our energy independence, which requires building sufficient inventory of fissile uranium233 through irradiation of thorium in thermal or fast nuclear reactors of relevant capacity. (Source: Kalpakkam fast breeder reactor attains criticality: why this is a ‘defining step’ in India’s 3-stage n-programme) What are the technological, economic, and safety challenges associated with fast breeder reactors? The fusion energy programme in India has steadily evolved over the past few decades. Mention India’s contributions to the international fusion energy project International Thermonuclear Experimental Reactor (ITER). What will be the implications of the success of this project for the future of global energy? (2025) With growing energy needs should India keep on expanding its nuclear energy programme? Discuss the facts and fears associated with nuclear energy? title: Trekker Sharanya survived dense forest for four days, but here's what worries her now - Onmanorama
content: Published: April 07, 2026 03:19 PM IST “I was only lost for a few days. Now that I am back, I can go trekking again,” says GS Sharanya, with a smile. Her mother, Shailaja, sighed with relief as she hugged her daughter tightly. Father Gopi and brother Syam also smiled, happy to have Sharanya back, safe and confident. Sharanya, who got lost in the Thadiyandamol peak in Coorg, was rescued by a team of forest officials, police and tribal people after four days. How did you plan this trip? I work as a software engineer in Kochi. I hadn’t applied for leave as it was a long weekend. I reached the place the previous day itself and learned that you need to book online for trekking. I booked a trek for the same night and got a slot between 6 am and 1 pm. I had planned to see around Coorg in the evening and then return to Kochi. I went for the trek carrying a few bananas and half a litre of water. I have been trekking for so long, but this is the first time that I got lost. Where did you lose the way? A father and son were walking in front of me within my view. But suddenly they couldn’t be spotted. Although I walked a bit further, no one could be seen. I saw a few people standing on top of another mountain. I was hoping I could reach the summit of that mountain if I climbed down the mountain where I was standing and then headed towards it on the left side. However, I realised that I was in a dense forest as I kept climbing down. I was sure that I got lost when my phone ran out of charge. How was the night inside the forest? I was so tired and fell asleep quickly. I realised that the forest is nothing like what we imagine at night. It was a full moon night, and the moonlight illuminated the forest. The crickets became silent by 7 pm, and then the forest was filled with fireflies. How did you manage? I had called the homestay and informed them before the call was disconnected. I screamed at intervals, hoping that someone would hear me. It rained profusely one day. On the fourth day, I decided to climb up. I heard my name being called as I climbed. They were a group of tribals. I had been drinking lots of water while staying close to the stream. I must have drunk at least three litres of water each day. I was able to stay healthy only because of that. I certainly lost some weight, but I wasn’t too tired. What did you learn from this experience? After the news of my getting lost in the forest spread, I am worried about only one thing. Girls who wish to go trekking may now be stopped, as my case illustrates. There is no need for all that. title: The Korea Land and Housing Corporation (LH) announced on the 7th that it signed an agreement with GS.. - 매일경제
content:
The Korea Land and Housing Corporation (LH) announced on the 7th that it signed an agreement with GS Engineering & Construction to implement a joint project to promote the public re-development project in Seongbuk District 1 with 2086 households. According to LH, the agreements include verifying the adequacy of construction costs, preventing excessive increases in construction costs, reflecting residents' intentions, linking prices, and reducing risks for private businesses. Seongbuk District 1 (area 179-68 Seongbuk-dong, Seongbuk-gu, Seongbuk-gu) has been struggling to promote the project for about 20 years since designation of the planned maintenance area in 2004, and the project was on track when LH was designated as a project implementer in October 2024. The area of the project district is 109,639.7㎡ and the construction cost is about 927.8 billion won. Earlier in March last year, LH carried out the process of selecting private businesses for joint project implementation after signing a project implementation agreement with the residents' representative meeting, and in December of the same year, GS Engineering & Construction was selected as the contractor through a general meeting of land owners. Based on the agreement, LH plans to speed up the project with the goal of approving the project implementation next year through procedures such as changing the maintenance plan within the year and integrated deliberation. Park Hyun-geun, head of LH Metropolitan City Improvement Special Division, said, "As Seongbuk District 1 has had difficulty in promoting redevelopment projects for a long time, we will make efforts to expedite the project based on this agreement." ※ This article was translated using AI technology for reader convenience. title: GS Plant Foods Announces Organic Liquid Soil Aerator For Eco-Friendly Lawn Care - markets.businessinsider.com
content:
In response to growing demand for organic gardening and landscaping products, GS Plant Foods has announced its Soft Soil liquid soil aerator, which is made from organic ingredients. Lake Mary, United States, April 6, 2026 -- GS Plant Foods has announced an organic, non-mechanical soil aerator, which joins a growing list of products in the organic gardening market—expected to expand as home gardeners and large-scale agricultural operations alike continue to adopt sustainable growing practices. With environmental concerns becoming more apparent to the public, GS Plant Foods hopes to provide consumers with additional eco-friendly and residue-free alternatives that will not pollute ecosystems. For more information, please visit https://gsplantfoods.com/collections/soil/products/softsoil According to Grand View Research, the lawn and gardening consumables market was valued at $23.10 billion USD in 2024 and is projected to reach $29.55 billion USD by 2030. Analysts with the company attribute growing demand to heightening interest in home gardening and landscaping for self-sufficiency and mental and physical health, and the shift from chemical fertilizers to more sustainable products highlights changing consumer preferences. Eco-friendly lawn maintenance, particularly lawn aeration, is one area that has gained attention in recent years. Gardening Know How says that lawn aeration is a vital practice that alleviates soil compaction, encouraging deeper root growth and allowing for more water and nutrient uptake. Traditionally, the practice is carried out with machinery that mechanically breaks the soil apart; however, organic liquid soil aeration products are also an option and may be more preferable to consumers who are unable to access soil aeration equipment. Organic liquid soil aerators, like GS Plant Foods’ Soft Soil, are surfactants that contain natural ingredients such as humic acid and kelp, which enhance aeration by stimulating beneficial microbes in the soil. These microbes accelerate decomposition, creating more organic matter and consequently improving water and nutrient retention without the need for machinery. Gardening experts with UK-based Online Turf say that liquid aerators are especially helpful for compacted or clay-like soils, as they add structure to the growing medium and boost soil health and microbiology. Liquid soil aerators can be applied with a watering can or a hose-end sprayer every two weeks during the growing season. Founded in 2018, GS Plant Foods is an online retailer that specializes in OMRI-certified organic gardening products, including fertilizers and soil amendments formulated to boost growth without harming the environment. title: GS Engineering & Construction Partners with Sampyo and Saint-Gobain for High-Rise Concrete Technology Development - asiae.co.kr
content:
GS Engineering & Construction Partners with Sampyo and Saint-Gobain for High-Rise Concrete Technology Development GS Engineering & Construction is joining forces with domestic and international specialized companies to collaborate on technology aimed at enhancing concrete pumping—a core technology for high-rise construction—as well as improving construction efficiency. On April 7, GS Engineering & Construction announced that it had signed a "High-Rise Construction Innovation Technology Cooperation" Memorandum of Understanding (MOU) with Sampyo Industrial, Sampyo Cement, and Saint-Gobain Korea Holdings (GCP Korea) at the GS Engineering & Construction R&D Center located in Seocho-dong. From left to right, Wonjin Lee, CEO of Sampyo Cement, Jongsuk Lee, CEO of Sampyo Industry, Seonghan Cho, Head of Future Technology Institute at GS Engineering & Construction, and Seokwoo Lee, CEO of Saint-Gobain Korea Holdings, are taking a commemorative photo. Provided by GS Engineering & Construction This MOU was pursued to improve the performance and construction stability of concrete pumping for high-rise buildings. Leveraging each company's technological capabilities, the partners will jointly carry out the entire process—from developing concrete mixes, verifying performance, conducting pilot construction, to on-site demonstrations. Through this technology development agreement for high-rise building construction, GS Engineering & Construction will establish performance evaluation standards for the new technologies and oversee the overall demonstration process, including pilot construction. Sampyo Industrial and Sampyo Cement will develop cement that achieves lower viscosity while maintaining strength and performance, and verify optimal mix designs and production technologies. Saint-Gobain Korea Holdings will be responsible for the development and optimization of admixtures, drawing on global high-rise application cases. GS Engineering & Construction plans to gradually apply the acquired technologies to major landmark projects, including the Han River Mansion reconstruction in Yongsan-gu, Seoul and the Citizens Park Promotion District 1 redevelopment in Busan, as well as future projects such as the Seongsu Strategic Maintenance District 1 redevelopment and the Yeouido Sambu reconstruction. A representative from GS Engineering & Construction stated, "The key to high-rise building construction is technology that enables stable concrete pumping to upper floors while ensuring strength and performance. Through this collaboration, we will advance every aspect of cement, mix design, admixture development, and constructability verification, thereby further strengthening GS Engineering & Construction's competitiveness in high-rise construction." © The Asia Business Daily(www.asiae.co.kr). title: Becky G’s lesson on protecting mental health against constant criticism and rumors - latination.com
content: Maintaining control over her personal situation in the face of third-party speculation constitutes a fundamental pillar for preserving mental peace in the digital environment. Prioritizing internal well-being over external approval defines the maturity of a career built under the constant scrutiny of social media. During her recent and acclaimed participation in the Call Her Daddy podcast, Becky G decided to shed her superstar armor to show the humanity that resides behind multi-platinum hits. In a profound talk with Alexandra Cooper, the singer of Mexican roots offered a masterclass on how to survive the toxicity of the digital era, transforming the pain of criticism and rumors into fuel for her own personal growth. Becky’s message serves as a reminder that success means nothing if one loses inner peace in the process. As she approaches a new stage in her career, Becky G reflected on what it means to grow up in front of the cameras. From her beginnings as a teenager with dreams on YouTube to becoming a global icon, exposure has remained her constant shadow. However, her main lesson teaches that hiding scars only makes them deeper. The artist emphasized that acknowledging her mistakes and sentimental failures does not make her weak; instead, it humanizes her figure before an audience that often forgets that celebrities also suffer. Faced with the controversies that usually flood the networks, she prefers to provide her own version of the facts to prevent outside interpretations from defining her life story. In a world of filters, Becky bets on transparency as the only way to build real bonds with her followers. Despite her resilience, the singer did not ignore the emotional weight that the infinite scrolling of negative comments carries. “Sometimes I feel that people forget that I am human,” she confessed, admitting that protecting her mental tranquility requires a conscious effort of disconnection and clear boundaries. Her participation in the program not only generated over half a million interactions in hours but also activated a necessary debate on self-care. By declaring that “vulnerability is a strength,” the artist invites her fans to seek help when necessary and not to surrender to social pressures. The closing of her intervention left a phrase that is already trending: “No negative comment defines my value or my story.” With this affirmation, Becky G closes a chapter of doubt to open one of emotional leadership, where she teaches that true victory lies not in streaming numbers, but in the ability to look in the mirror and recognize oneself with love, regardless of what the digital world may say. title: GS E&C partners with Sampyo and GCP Korea to advance ultra-high-rise concrete - CHOSUNBIZ - Chosunbiz
content: GS Engineering & Construction said on the 7th that it signed a memorandum of understanding (MOU) with Sampyo Industry, SAMPYO Cement, and Saint-Gobain Korea Holdings (GCP Korea) for "ultra-high-rise construction innovation technology cooperation." The heart of the agreement is to develop low-viscosity, high-flow concrete technology that can easily transport mixed cement to ultra-high-rise levels. It also aims to place concrete stably in the upper floors of ultra-high-rise buildings by developing technology that secures strength and performance. Under the agreement, GS Engineering & Construction will establish performance evaluation standards for the developed technology and oversee overall demonstration, including pilot construction. Sampyo Industry and SAMPYO Cement will develop cement that lowers viscosity while securing strength and performance, and verify optimal mix design and production technology. GCP Korea will handle the development and optimization of admixtures based on concrete application cases in global ultra-high-rise buildings. GS Engineering & Construction said, "Once the technology is developed, it will contribute not only to improved constructability but also to enhanced quality stability and shorter construction periods." title: GS Caltex and Veolia partner to drive global sustainability solutions - Indian Chemical News
content: MoU signing follows French President Emmanuel Macron’s visit to Korea L-R: GS Caltex CEO Saehong Hur; Veolia Senior Executive Vice President of Business Performance Christophe Maquet GS Caltex had signed a comprehensive memorandum of understanding (MOU) with Veolia to innovate utility operations and strengthen sustainability at its Yeosu Complex. The partnership will focus on developing and implementing integrated solutions across environmental, energy, and digital sectors. This will be pursued through the Triple-X framework, encompassing Business Transformation (BX), Digital & AI Transformation (DAX), and Green Transformation (GX). The two companies signed the MoU following the visit of French President Emmanuel Macron to Korea, expanding their relationship from a limited, project-based collaboration in water treatment to a broader partnership aimed at sharing future strategies. Headquartered in Paris, France, Veolia is the world’s foremost company in water, waste, and energy management. Under BX, the partnership will explore ways to integrate and optimize wastewater treatment operations at the Yeosu Complex as well as opportunities to enhance utility synergies with nearby companies. These efforts are expected to maximize operational efficiency and bolster competitiveness. DAX, a key strategic priority for GS Caltex, will see the introduction of Veolia’s AI-based digital solutions at the Yeosu Complex to refine real-time analysis of utility operations data and improve intelligent process management systems. By leveraging these capabilities, GS Caltex will accelerate the transition to a smart plant through process optimization, anomaly detection, and predictive maintenance. For GX, focused on sustainability, GS Caltex will collaborate with Veolia to adopt the latter’s proprietary eco-friendly technologies at the Yeosu Complex, including wastewater reuse, cooling system upgrades, and resource recovery. The approach is intended to reduce carbon emissions and solidify the foundation for a circular economy. GS Caltex Board Chair & CEO, GS Caltex / Vice Chairman, GS Group, Saehong Hur said: “Our collaboration with Veolia will serve as a catalyst for transforming our overall utility operations and elevating our ESG management to the next level,” adding, “We will strengthen our sustainable competitiveness based on world-class technology and operational expertise.” From Policy To Performance: How Lubrizol Additives Supports India's Accelerated E20 Journey Natco Pharma – Accelerating Regulatory Readiness with Revvity Signals Catalyzing Smart Growth with Industrial Intelligence The Sprint to the Summit: Unlocking Lab Efficiency through Digital Transformation Forbes Marshall - Need For Ongoing Improvement, Optimisation And Sustenance Delivering on the Promise of Prescriptive Maintenance The Sprint to the Summit: Unlocking Lab Efficiency through Digital Transformation The guiding role of fluidized and spouted bed technologies in particle building processes Grupa Azoty sells GA Polyolefins for PLN 1.183 billion to Orlen EIB and Eni partner on €500 million biorefinery conversion in Italy Rohm hits full capacity at Texas MMA plant to showcase breakthrough LiMA technology Perpetual Next taps Bilfinger to power landmark biomethanol project at Moerdijk ACC and OSHA forge powerful new alliance to boost worker safety nationwide Green hydrogen in India - from policy to action: Ajay Mathur, Professor, School of Public Policy, IIT Delhi & Former Director General, International Solar Alliance GS Caltex and Veolia partner to drive global sustainability solutions Colonial Chemical Solutions expands reach with acquisition of Integrity Partners Group NH3 Clean Energy taps Linde for landmark WAH2 ammonia project FEED South Africa lands major green fuel boost with Honeywell deal Subscribe to our newsletter & stay updated. title: The ‘violent’ reality of ejecting from an F-15 faced by brave US pilots revealed: ‘Instantaneous 10 to 20 Gs of force’ - New York Post
content: A Navy veteran pulled back the curtain on the “violent” experience airmen like the ones on the downed F-15E endure when ejecting from an aircraft — revealing the emergency maneuver includes little to no parachute training. Matthew “Whiz” Buckley, a Topgun graduate and president of the No Fallen Heroes Foundation, told The Post that the Air Force colonel and his pilot made “a pretty stark choice to either die or eject” last week when they were shot down over Iran. Few would know better than Buckley, who piloted F-18 Hornets in 44 combat missions during two tours in Iraq. “You’re always worried about the condition of the aviator and aircrew, if they had to eject, because ejecting is one of the most violent experiences a body can go through,” Buckley explained, noting that no human is built to withstand “instantaneous 10 to 20 Gs of force.” Buckley described the “rocket ride” combined with “500 miles an hour of wind blast” as a recipe for disaster if the ejector’s body isn’t in the perfect position. If any of the occupant’s limbs are the slightest bit loose, they can sustain a flail injury that “might just” tear their arms out of their sockets, Buckley warned. The seconds-long process requires a daunting level of precision, but Buckley said that the airmen “don’t train” for it. “A lot of people think that, oh yeah, if you’re a fighter pilot, you must do some parachute training. Zero,” Buckley said. He noted that there is such a thing as a “brutal” ejection seat trainer — but it pales in comparison to “the real thing.” The Air Force colonel was seriously injured, but still managed to evade enemy forces while hiding in the Zagros Mountains for a day and a half until he was rescued on Easter Sunday. While it’s not clear what kind of injuries he sustained, Buckley suggested he was likely banged up either during the whirlwind ejection or after landing on “pretty unforgiving-looking territory.” Typically, he said, the compression from the ejection itself can cause things like spinal cord injuries. He noted that, in the early days of aerial combat, some soldiers would lose their legs or feet “because they got stuck on the canopy or on the rudder pedals” when the ejection was triggered. So long as the majority of the airman’s body made it out of the plane, such ejections would be hailed as a triumph. “The military defines a successful ejection as pilot pulls the ejection handle. Canopy fires, rocket fires, parachute opens. What didn’t I name? You live,” Buckley said. “So the definition of a successful ejection to the military is all that stuff worked. What happened to the pilot is in God’s hands,” he added. The heroic airman said “God is good” over the radio when he finally reached a hiding place in the Zargros Mountains — which almost tripped up President Trump, who speculated that his prayer “sounded like something a Muslim would say.” The colonel and his pilot, who was rescued just hours after their F-15E jet was shot down on Friday, are both in stable condition, Trump told reporters on Monday.

title: Is It Too Late To Consider Goldman Sachs Group (GS) After Its 89% One Year Surge? - Yahoo Finance
content: Tip: Try a valid symbol or a specific company name for relevant results Track your investments for FREE with Simply Wall St, the portfolio command center trusted by over 7 million individual investors worldwide. Investors may be wondering whether Goldman Sachs Group, at around US$863 per share, is still a sensible entry or hold, or if most of the easy gains are already on the table. The stock has moved 6.9% over the last 7 days and 5.1% over the last 30 days. The 1 year return sits at 89.2%, while the 3 and 5 year returns are 185.8% and 193.3% respectively, with a year-to-date return of a 5.6% decline. Recent coverage has focused on how a major global investment bank like Goldman Sachs Group fits into a market that is still reacting to shifting rates, regulation and capital markets activity. Headlines have highlighted its role in financing, trading and advisory activity, which helps explain why investors are closely watching the stock after such strong multi-year returns. Simply Wall St currently assigns Goldman Sachs Group a valuation score of 4 out of 6. The sections that follow will break that down across different valuation methods before circling back to a broader way to think about what the market is really pricing in. Goldman Sachs Group delivered 89.2% returns over the last year. See how this stacks up to the rest of the Capital Markets industry. The Excess Returns model looks at how much value Goldman Sachs Group generates above the return that equity investors are assumed to require. It starts from the book value of the business and compares the returns on that equity to the cost of equity. For Goldman Sachs Group, book value is estimated at $356.47 per share, with a stable book value of $402.19 per share based on weighted future book value estimates from 14 analysts. Stable EPS is estimated at $67.46 per share, sourced from weighted future return on equity estimates from 13 analysts. The average return on equity is 16.77% and the cost of equity is $37.03 per share, which results in an excess return of $30.42 per share. Simply Wall St aggregates these excess returns over time and discounts them to today, producing an intrinsic value estimate of about $926.90 per share. Compared with the recent share price of around $863, this implies the stock is 6.9% undervalued, which is a relatively modest gap. Goldman Sachs Group is fairly valued according to our Excess Returns, but this can change at a moment's notice. Track the value in your watchlist or portfolio and be alerted on when to act. Head to the Valuation section of our Company Report for more details on how we arrive at this Fair Value for Goldman Sachs Group. For a profitable company like Goldman Sachs Group, the P/E ratio is a useful shorthand for how much you are paying for each dollar of earnings. It helps you quickly compare what the market is willing to pay for this earnings stream versus other banks and the broader Capital Markets industry. What counts as a “normal” or “fair” P/E depends on how the market views a company’s growth prospects and risk. Higher expected growth or lower perceived risk can justify a higher P/E, while lower growth or higher risk usually aligns with a lower multiple. Goldman Sachs Group currently trades on a P/E of 16.29x. That is below the Capital Markets industry average of 36.13x and below the peer group average of 24.80x. Simply Wall St also calculates a Fair Ratio of 18.11x, which is the P/E that would typically be expected given factors like Goldman Sachs Group’s earnings profile, industry, profit margins, market cap and specific risks. This Fair Ratio can be more informative than a simple peer or industry comparison because it is tailored to the company’s own fundamentals rather than broad group averages. With the current 16.29x P/E sitting below the 18.11x Fair Ratio, the shares appear moderately undervalued on this metric. P/E ratios tell one story, but what if the real opportunity lies elsewhere? For Goldman Sachs Group however, we will make it really easy for you with previews of two leading Goldman Sachs Group Narratives: Fair value in this bullish narrative: US$959.75 per share. Implied undervaluation versus the recent US$863 price: about 10.1%. Advisory and asset and wealth management are expected to carry more of the load, with a tilt toward fee based and higher margin revenue streams. Analysts in this camp see AI, automation and more capital light financing businesses supporting return on equity and long term earnings. The thesis depends on steady M&A activity, ongoing demand for alternatives, and a supportive regulatory backdrop that allows continued buybacks and a rising dividend stream. Fair value in this bearish narrative: US$726.27 per share. Implied overvaluation versus the recent US$863 price: about 18.8%. This view focuses on pressure from digital competitors, fee compression and heavier regulation that could keep margins under strain across several business lines. It assumes more modest revenue growth and a lower future P/E multiple, with earnings still leaning heavily on cyclical trading and investment banking. Supporters of this narrative see the current price as embedding optimistic expectations for dividends and buybacks that may be hard to sustain if those headwinds play out. Both narratives use explicit assumptions for revenue, margins, capital returns and discount rates. title: Air Jordan 4 GS "Black Infrared 23" Releases May 2026 - Sneaker Files
content: The Air Jordan 4 GS “Black Infrared 23” will release on May 1, 2026. A grade-school exclusive colorway built for younger sneakerheads, this pair expands the Air Jordan 4’s already packed 2026 lineup. The retail price is $165. Air Jordan 4 GS “Infrared 23”Color: Black/Bright Mango-Barely Volt-Infrared 23Release Date: May 1, 2026Style Code: II1271-001Price: $165Where to Buy: Nike, Foot Locker, DSG, Finish Line, Hibbett, ShiekhMore Info: Air Jordan Release Dates Jordan Brand’s Summer 2026 calendar keeps delivering, and this next one is for the kids, though plenty of adults are going to want it in their size, too. Known as the “Black Infrared 23” Air Jordan 4, the look doesn’t scream “kids’ exclusive,” which is why I feel the brand should have dropped these in adult sizes as well. Jordan Brand has made a clear effort this year to give younger collectors their own chapter of the Air Jordan 4 story, and this new colorway should be a hit. This Air Jordan 4 is dressed in a Black, Bright Mango, Barely Volt, and Infrared 23 color combination. Highlighted with a Black suede upper. Jordan Brand adds energy with Barely Volt accents on the mesh netting and tongue, making the shoe feel more vibrant. The real standout comes lower on the shoe, where Bright Mango and Infrared 23 work together across the midsole and outsole. Furthermore, the midsole has speckled details. Lastly, Jumpman branding is throughout, while the tongue label features water droplets. The Air Jordan 4 GS “Black Infrared 23” will be released on May 1st, 2026, at select Jordan Brand retailers, including Nike, Foot Locker, DSG, Finish Line, Hibbett, and Shiekh. The retail price is $165 in grade school sizes. Below, you can check out more photos for a closer look. This pair will stand out for sure, and since I can see some adults wanting to pick it up, it’s a pass for me. The colorway is a bit vibrant for my taste as of late, although I do like it when the brand uses Infrared. I think the addition of Barely Volt and Mango is what throws it off. But if you like them, don’t let me talk you out of buying. No, this colorway was released exclusively in kids’ sizes. It’s a great option for younger sneaker fans or anyone looking for a versatile, everyday Jordan 4 with a bit of color pop. Your email address will not be published. title: GS Caltex signs strategic partnership with Veolia to enhance utilities and water treatment - chemengonline.com
content:
Please contact clientservices@accessintel.com
		or call 1-888-707-5814 if you are unable to login. GS Caltex (Seoul, South Korea) ) announced that it had signed a comprehensive memorandum of understanding (MOU) with Veolia (Paris, France), a global environmental solutions company,  to innovate utility operations and strengthen sustainability at its Yeosu Complex. The two companies signed the MOU following the visit of French President Emmanuel Macron to Korea, expanding their relationship from a limited, project-based collaboration in water treatment to a broader partnership aimed at sharing future strategies. GS Caltex had signed a comprehensive memorandum of understanding (MOU) with Veolia to innovate utility operations and strengthen sustainability at its Yeosu Complex. (From left: GS Caltex CEO Saehong Hur; Veolia Senior Executive Vice President of Business Performance Christophe Maquet) (source: GS Caltex) To address tightening environmental regulations and growing demands for energy efficiency, the partnership will focus on developing and implementing integrated solutions across environmental, energy, and digital sectors. This will be pursued through the Triple-X framework, encompassing Business Transformation (BX), Digital & AI Transformation (DAX), and Green Transformation (GX). Under BX, the partnership will explore ways to integrate and optimize wastewater treatment operations at the Yeosu Complex, as well as opportunities to enhance utility synergies with nearby companies. These efforts are expected to maximize operational efficiency and bolster competitiveness. DAX, a key strategic priority for GS Caltex, will see the introduction of Veolia’s AI-based digital solutions at the Yeosu Complex to refine real-time analysis of utility operations data and improve intelligent process management systems. By leveraging these capabilities, GS Caltex will accelerate the transition to a smart plant through process optimization, anomaly detection, and predictive maintenance. For GX, focused on sustainability, GS Caltex will collaborate with Veolia to adopt the latter’s proprietary eco-friendly technologies at the Yeosu Complex, including wastewater reuse, cooling system upgrades, and resource recovery. The approach is intended to reduce carbon emissions and solidify the foundation for a circular economy. CEO Saehong Hur said, “Our collaboration with Veolia will serve as a catalyst for transforming our overall utility operations and elevating our ESG management to the next level,” adding, “We will strengthen our sustainable competitiveness based on world-class technology and operational expertise.” Chemical Engineering news and content delivered every week © Access Intelligence, LLC.

title: Why Goldman Sachs (GS) is a Top Dividend Stock for Your Portfolio - Yahoo Finance
content: Tip: Try a valid symbol or a specific company name for relevant results Getting big returns from financial portfolios, whether through stocks, bonds, ETFs, other securities, or a combination of all, is an investor's dream. But when you're an income investor, your primary focus is generating consistent cash flow from each of your liquid investments. While cash flow can come from bond interest or interest from other types of investments, income investors hone in on dividends. A dividend is the distribution of a company's earnings paid out to shareholders; it's often viewed by its dividend yield, a metric that measures a dividend as a percent of the current stock price. Many academic studies show that dividends account for significant portions of long-term returns, with dividend contributions exceeding one-third of total returns in many cases. Goldman Sachs (GS) is headquartered in New York, and is in the Finance sector. The stock has seen a price change of -1.82% since the start of the year. The investment bank is currently shelling out a dividend of $4.50 per share, with a dividend yield of 2.09%. This compares to the Financial - Investment Bank industry's yield of 1.2% and the S&P 500's yield of 1.47%. Looking at dividend growth, the company's current annualized dividend of $18.00 is up 28.6% from last year. Over the last 5 years, Goldman Sachs has increased its dividend 4 times on a year-over-year basis for an average annual increase of 22.04%. Looking ahead, future dividend growth will be dependent on earnings growth and payout ratio, which is the proportion of a company's annual earnings per share that it pays out as a dividend. Goldman's current payout ratio is 31%, meaning it paid out 31% of its trailing 12-month EPS as dividend. Earnings growth looks solid for GS for this fiscal year. The Zacks Consensus Estimate for 2026 is $57.86 per share, with earnings expected to increase 12.74% from the year ago period. Investors like dividends for many reasons; they greatly improve stock investing profits, decrease overall portfolio risk, and carry tax advantages, among others. But, not every company offers a quarterly payout. Big, established firms that have more secure profits are often seen as the best dividend options, but it's fairly uncommon to see high-growth businesses or tech start-ups offer their stockholders a dividend. During periods of rising interest rates, income investors must be mindful that high-yielding stocks tend to struggle. With that in mind, GS is a compelling investment opportunity. Not only is it a strong dividend play, but the stock currently sits at a Zacks Rank of #3 (Hold). Want the latest recommendations from Zacks Investment Research? title: BMO Capital Markets Lowers The Goldman Sachs Group (NYSE:GS) Price Target to $905.00 - MarketBeat
content:
The Goldman Sachs Group (NYSE:GS - Get Free Report) had its price target dropped by equities research analysts at BMO Capital Markets  from $985.00 to $905.00 in a note issued to investors on Monday,MarketScreener reports. The brokerage currently has a "market perform" rating on the investment management company's stock. BMO Capital Markets' price objective would suggest a potential upside of 4.42% from the stock's current price. Other equities research analysts have also issued reports about the company. Finally, Wells Fargo & Company raised their target price on shares of The Goldman Sachs Group from $970.00 to $1,050.00 and gave the stock an "overweight" rating in a report on Thursday, January 15th. The business's revenue was down 3.0% compared to the same quarter last year. During the same period in the prior year, the company earned $11.95 EPS.  Analysts anticipate that  The Goldman Sachs Group will post 47.12 earnings per share for the current fiscal year. title: Goldman Sachs (GS) Earnings Expected to Grow: What to Know Ahead of Next Week's Release - Yahoo Finance
content: Tip: Try a valid symbol or a specific company name for relevant results Wall Street expects a year-over-year increase in earnings on higher revenues when Goldman Sachs (GS) reports results for the quarter ended March 2026. While this widely-known consensus outlook is important in gauging the company's earnings picture, a powerful factor that could impact its near-term stock price is how the actual results compare to these estimates. The stock might move higher if these key numbers top expectations in the upcoming earnings report, which is expected to be released on April 13. On the other hand, if they miss, the stock may move lower. While the sustainability of the immediate price change and future earnings expectations will mostly depend on management's discussion of business conditions on the earnings call, it's worth handicapping the probability of a positive EPS surprise. This investment bank is expected to post quarterly earnings of $16.48 per share in its upcoming report, which represents a year-over-year change of +16.7%. Revenues are expected to be $17.01 billion, up 12.9% from the year-ago quarter. The consensus EPS estimate for the quarter has been revised 3.65% lower over the last 30 days to the current level. This is essentially a reflection of how the covering analysts have collectively reassessed their initial estimates over this period. Investors should keep in mind that the direction of estimate revisions by each of the covering analysts may not always get reflected in the aggregate change. Estimate revisions ahead of a company's earnings release offer clues to the business conditions for the period whose results are coming out. This insight is at the core of our proprietary surprise prediction model -- the Zacks Earnings ESP (Expected Surprise Prediction). The Zacks Earnings ESP compares the Most Accurate Estimate to the Zacks Consensus Estimate for the quarter; the Most Accurate Estimate is a more recent version of the Zacks Consensus EPS estimate. The idea here is that analysts revising their estimates right before an earnings release have the latest information, which could potentially be more accurate than what they and others contributing to the consensus had predicted earlier. Thus, a positive or negative Earnings ESP reading theoretically indicates the likely deviation of the actual earnings from the consensus estimate. However, the model's predictive power is significant for positive ESP readings only. A positive Earnings ESP is a strong predictor of an earnings beat, particularly when combined with a Zacks Rank #1 (Strong Buy), 2 (Buy) or 3 (Hold). Our research shows that stocks with this combination produce a positive surprise nearly 70% of the time, and a solid Zacks Rank actually increases the predictive power of Earnings ESP. Please note that a negative Earnings ESP reading is not indicative of an earnings miss. Our research shows that it is difficult to predict an earnings beat with any degree of confidence for stocks with negative Earnings ESP readings and/or Zacks Rank of 4 (Sell) or 5 (Strong Sell). For Goldman, the Most Accurate Estimate is higher than the Zacks Consensus Estimate, suggesting that analysts have recently become bullish on the company's earnings prospects. This has resulted in an Earnings ESP of +3.36%. On the other hand, the stock currently carries a Zacks Rank of #3. So, this combination indicates that Goldman will most likely beat the consensus EPS estimate. While calculating estimates for a company's future earnings, analysts often consider to what extent it has been able to match past consensus estimates. So, it's worth taking a look at the surprise history for gauging its influence on the upcoming number. For the last reported quarter, it was expected that Goldman would post earnings of $11.77 per share when it actually produced earnings of $14.01, delivering a surprise of +19.03%. Over the last four quarters, the company has beaten consensus EPS estimates four times. An earnings beat or miss may not be the sole basis for a stock moving higher or lower. Many stocks end up losing ground despite an earnings beat due to other factors that disappoint investors. Similarly, unforeseen catalysts help a number of stocks gain despite an earnings miss. That said, betting on stocks that are expected to beat earnings expectations does increase the odds of success. This is why it's worth checking a company's Earnings ESP and Zacks Rank ahead of its quarterly release. Make sure to utilize our Earnings ESP Filter to uncover the best stocks to buy or sell before they've reported. Goldman appears a compelling earnings-beat candidate. However, investors should pay attention to other factors too for betting on this stock or staying away from it ahead of its earnings release. For beginners, it can seem like a good idea (and an exciting prospect) to buy a company that tells a good story to investors, even if it currently lacks a track record of revenue and profit.  Sometimes these stories can cloud the minds of investors, leading them to invest with their emotions rather than on the merit of good company fundamentals.  A loss-making company is yet to prove itself with profit, and eventually the inflow of external capital may dry up. If this kind of company isn't your style, you like companies that generate revenue, and even earn profits, then you may well be interested in Goldman Sachs Group (NYSE:GS). Generally, companies experiencing growth in earnings per share (EPS) should see similar trends in share price.  That makes EPS growth an attractive quality for any company.   It certainly is nice to see that Goldman Sachs Group has managed to grow EPS by 20% per year over three years.   As a general rule, we'd say that if a company can keep up that sort of growth, shareholders will be beaming. It's often helpful to take a look at earnings before interest and tax (EBIT) margins, as well as revenue growth, to get another take on the quality of the company's growth.   It's noted that Goldman Sachs Group's revenue from operations was lower than its revenue in the last twelve months, so that could distort our analysis of its margins.     Goldman Sachs Group maintained stable EBIT margins over the last year, all while growing revenue 14% to US$59b.  That's encouraging news for the company! We would not expect to see insiders owning a large percentage of a US$265b company like Goldman Sachs Group.  But we are reassured by the fact they have invested in the company.     Indeed, they have a considerable amount of wealth invested in it, currently valued at US$1.2b.   While that is a lot of skin in the game, we note this holding only totals to 0.4% of the business, which is a result of the company being so large.  This should still be a great incentive for management to maximise shareholder value. If you believe that share price follows earnings per share you should definitely be delving further into Goldman Sachs Group's strong EPS growth.   This EPS growth rate is something the company should be proud of, and so it's no surprise that insiders are holding on to a considerable chunk of shares.  On the balance of its merits, solid EPS growth and company insiders who are aligned with the shareholders would indicate a business that is worthy of further research.
